11	// newWorkCommand creates the top-level "ddx work" command: the FEAT-010 layer-3
12	// queue drain. Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness,
13	// --provider, and --model as opaque passthrough constraints that are forwarded
14	// to the agent unchanged. DDx does not validate or branch on their values.
15	// Retry-power policy between attempts is owned here, not in the agent service.
16	func (f *CommandFactory) newWorkCommand() *cobra.Command {
17		cmd := &cobra.Command{
18			Use:   "work",
19			Short: "Drain the bead execution queue",
20			Long: `work drains the execution-ready bead queue. It is the FEAT-010 layer-3
21	queue drain: it iterates ddx try (layer 2) across ready beads until a stop
22	condition is met and owns retry-power policy between attempts.
23	
24	Unlike "ddx agent execute-loop", ddx work treats --harness, --provider, and
25	--model as opaque passthrough constraints forwarded to the agent unchanged.
26	DDx does not validate these values or branch on them; the agent owns routing
27	within the requested power bounds.
28	
29	Stop conditions (evaluated between attempts):
30	  drained     — no ready beads remain
31	  blocked     — every remaining bead has produced a terminal non-success outcome
32	  deferred    — configured budget exhausted
33	  no_progress — N consecutive attempts produced no commit (default N=3)
34	  signal      — SIGINT/SIGTERM received between attempts
35	
36	work runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no separate
37	"submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but ignored
38	(deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
39	`,
40			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit
41	  ddx work
42	
43	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
44	  ddx work --once
45	
46	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker
47	  ddx work --poll-interval 30s
48	
49	  # Forward harness/model as passthrough constraints (ddx does not validate these)
50	  ddx work --once --harness agent --model minimax/minimax-m2.7
51	
52	  # Constrain power tier (retry-power policy is owned by ddx work)
53	  ddx work --once --min-power 40 --max-power 90`,
54			Args: cobra.NoArgs,
55			RunE: f.runWork,
56		}
57	
58		// Routing knobs — passthrough only. ddx work forwards these to the agent
59		// unchanged and does not validate or branch on their string values.
60		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
61		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
62		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
63		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
64		cmd.Flags().String("profile", agent.DefaultRoutingProfile, "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart")
65		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx work does not validate)")
66		cmd.Flags().String("model-ref", "", "Model catalog reference (e.g. code-medium); resolved via the model catalog")
67		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
68		cmd.Flags().Bool("once", false, "Process at most one ready bead")
69		cmd.Flags().Duration("poll-interval", 30*time.Second, "Poll interval for continuous scanning; zero drains current ready work and exits (legacy opt-out). Default 30s keeps the worker alive across empty polls.")
70		cmd.Flags().Bool("json", false, "Output loop result as JSON")
71		cmd.Flags().Bool("local", false, "Deprecated: no-op; ddx work always runs inline (ADR-022)")
72		_ = cmd.Flags().MarkDeprecated("local", "ddx work always runs inline; the flag is a no-op (ADR-022)")
73		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review")
74		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
75		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
76		cmd.Flags().Float64("max-cost", escalation.DefaultMaxCostUSD, "Stop when accumulated billed cost exceeds USD; 0 = unlimited")
77		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
78		// Power bounds: ddx work owns retry-power policy — these set the envelope
79		// for operator-constrained power within which the agent selects the route.
80		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
81		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained); passed to agent routing unchanged")
82	
83		return cmd
84	}

86	// runWork is the RunE for "ddx work". It drives the FEAT-010 layer-3 queue
87	// drain with harness/provider/model treated as opaque passthrough constraints —
88	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
89	// branch on those values. Retry-power policy (min-power / max-power) is owned
90	// by this layer.
91	func (f *CommandFactory) runWork(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
92		return f.runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd, true)
93	}

13	// TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsProfileLadders verifies AC #4 of bead ddx-3bd7396a:
14	// a project config carrying agent.routing.profile_ladders causes ddx work
15	// --once --local to fail with a hard error naming the removed field.
16	func TestExecuteLoopLocalRejectsProfileLadders(t *testing.T) {
17		dir := t.TempDir()
18		ddxDir := filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx")
19		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
20		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "config.yaml"), []byte(`version: "1.0"
21	library:
22	  path: ".ddx/plugins/ddx"
23	  repository:
24	    url: "https://example.com/lib"
25	    branch: "main"
26	agent:
27	  routing:
28	    profile_ladders:
29	      default: [cheap, standard, smart]
30	`), 0o644))
31	
32		factory := NewCommandFactory(dir)
33		root := factory.NewRootCommand()
34		_, err := executeCommand(root, "agent", "execute-loop", "--json", "--once")
35		require.Error(t, err, "execute-loop must fail when config has profile_ladders")
36		// routinglint:legacy-rejection reason="asserts the rejection error names the retired field"
37		assert.Contains(t, err.Error(), "profile_ladders")
38	}

12	// TestWorkCommandHasPassthroughFlags verifies that ddx work exposes the
13	// harness/provider/model/min-power/max-power passthrough flags that operators
14	// use to constrain agent routing. These flags are forwarded opaquely; ddx work
15	// does not validate or branch on their string values.
16	func TestWorkCommandHasPassthroughFlags(t *testing.T) {
17		dir := t.TempDir()
18		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
19	
20		workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
21		require.NoError(t, err, "ddx work must exist")
22		require.NotNil(t, workCmd)
23	
24		for _, name := range []string{"harness", "provider", "model", "min-power", "max-power"} {
25			f := workCmd.Flags().Lookup(name)
26			assert.NotNil(t, f, "ddx work must have --%s passthrough flag", name)
27		}
28	}
29	
30	// TestWorkCommandHasAllExecuteLoopFlags verifies that ddx work exposes the
31	// full set of flags that operators need to control queue drain behavior.
32	// All execute-loop flags must be present so operators can use work as the
33	// primary queue drain surface without switching commands.
34	func TestWorkCommandHasAllExecuteLoopFlags(t *testing.T) {
35		dir := t.TempDir()
36		root := NewCommandFactory(dir).NewRootCommand()
37	
38		workCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"work"})
39		require.NoError(t, err, "ddx work must exist")
40	
41		loopCmd, _, err := root.Find([]string{"agent", "execute-loop"})
42		require.NoError(t, err, "ddx agent execute-loop must exist")
43	
44		loopFlags := map[string]bool{}
45		loopCmd.Flags().VisitAll(func(f *pflag.Flag) {
46			loopFlags[f.Name] = true
47		})
48	
49		for name := range loopFlags {
50			if workCmd.Flags().Lookup(name) == nil {
51				t.Errorf("ddx work missing flag --%s from execute-loop", name)
52			}
53		}
54	}

56	// TestWorkPassthroughNotValidated is the primary AC test: ddx work must NOT
57	// call ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService. When an unknown harness is provided
58	// and no ready beads exist, work must succeed with no_ready_work rather than
59	// failing on harness validation. This proves harness/provider/model are opaque
60	// passthrough — ddx work does not validate or branch on their string values.
61	func TestWorkPassthroughNotValidated(t *testing.T) {
62		env := NewTestEnvironment(t)
63		root := NewCommandFactory(env.Dir).NewRootCommand()
64	
65		// "bogus_harness_xyz_notreal" does not exist in any harness registry.
66		// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService would fail with "unknown harness: ..."
67		// if called. ddx work must skip that call and proceed to the queue scan,
68		// finding no ready beads and returning successfully.
69		out, err := executeCommand(root, "work",
70			"--json", "--once",
71			"--harness=bogus_harness_xyz_notreal",
72		)
73		require.NoError(t, err,
74			"ddx work must not fail on unknown harness when there are no ready beads; "+
75				"harness is opaque passthrough, not validated by ddx work")
76	
77		var res struct {
78			NoReadyWork bool `json:"no_ready_work"`
79		}
80		require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(out), &res))
81		assert.True(t, res.NoReadyWork,
82			"ddx work with no ready beads must report no_ready_work=true")
83	}

26		// Explicit --poll-interval=0 selects legacy drain-and-exit semantics

28		// across empty polls; tests that want to assert the no-ready-work
29		// outcome must opt out of the long-running default.

13	// TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified covers ddx-b790449b AC1
14	// and AC2: a project with .ddx/beads.jsonl but no .ddx/config.yaml must drain
15	// against the global agent config defaults instead of failing with the
16	// upstream "routing under-specified" error. The test sets HOME to a temp
17	// directory holding a minimal agent config and runs `ddx work --once`
18	// programmatically. It asserts:
19	//   - the command does not return "routing under-specified" (the regression
20	//     this hot-fix addresses)
21	//   - no .ddx/config.yaml is created as a side effect of the run
22	//
23	// Real provider dispatch is not exercised: the bead's actual execution may
24	// fail because the temp config points at an unreachable endpoint, but that
25	// failure is downstream of the routing decision and unrelated to AC1.
26	func TestZeroConfigWork_NoConfigDoesNotEmitUnderSpecified(t *testing.T) {
27		projectDir := t.TempDir()
28		ddxDir := filepath.Join(projectDir, ".ddx")
29		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddxDir, 0o755))
30	
31		// Single trivial open bead so the loop has something to route.
32		beads := `{"id":"test-zero-cfg-1","title":"trivial","type":"task","status":"open","priority":3,"created_at":"2026-05-01T00:00:00Z","updated_at":"2026-05-01T00:00:00Z"}` + "\n"
33		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(ddxDir, "beads.jsonl"), []byte(beads), 0o644))
34	
35		// Synthesize a HOME with a minimal global agent config so the
36		// providersHasProviders precheck passes. The provider points at a
37		// localhost port no test will ever bind — actual dispatch will fail
38		// but the routing-under-specified gate must not.
39		homeDir := t.TempDir()
40		agentCfgDir := filepath.Join(homeDir, ".config", "agent")
41		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(agentCfgDir, 0o755))
42		agentCfg := `providers:

1503			Long: `execute-loop is the primary queue-driven execution surface. It scans the
1504	target project's execution-ready bead queue, claims the next ready bead,
1505	runs "ddx agent execute-bead" on it from the project root, records the
1506	structured result, and continues until no unattempted ready work remains.
1507	
1508	Reach for execute-loop by default. Use "ddx agent execute-bead" directly
1509	only as the primitive for debugging or re-running one specific bead.
1510	
1511	Planning and document-only beads are valid execution targets — any bead
1512	with unmet acceptance criteria and no blocking deps is eligible.
1513	
1514	Close semantics (per execute-bead result status):
1515	  success                      — close bead with session + commit evidence
1516	  already_satisfied            — close bead (after repeated no_changes)
1517	  no_changes                   — unclaim; may cooldown or close after retries
1518	  land_conflict                — unclaim; result preserved under refs/ddx/iterations/
1519	  post_run_check_failed        — unclaim; result preserved
1520	  execution_failed             — unclaim
1521	  structural_validation_failed — unclaim
1522	
1523	Only success (and already_satisfied) closes the bead. Every other status
1524	leaves the bead open and unclaimed so a later attempt can try again. Each
1525	attempt is appended to the bead as an execute-bead event (status, detail,
1526	base_rev, result_rev, preserve_ref, retry_after), and the underlying agent
1527	session log is recorded under the execute-bead agent-log path.
1528	
1529	execute-loop runs inline in the current process; per ADR-022 there is no
1530	separate "submit to server" mode. The legacy --local flag is accepted but
1531	ignored (deprecation warning printed) and will be removed in a future release.
1532	
1533	Project targeting (multi-project servers):
1534	  --project <path>    target a specific project root (absolute path or name)
1535	  DDX_PROJECT_ROOT    env var fallback; used when --project is not set
1536	  (default)           the git root of the current working directory
1537	
1538	When submitting to a multi-project server you must ensure the target project
1539	is registered with the server (run "ddx server" from that directory, or use
1540	"ddx server projects register"). The server rejects unrecognised project paths.
1541	`,
1542			Example: `  # Drain the current execution-ready queue once and exit (normal surface)
1543	  ddx agent execute-loop
1544	
1545	  # Pick one ready bead, execute it, and stop
1546	  ddx agent execute-loop --profile default --once

1548	  # Run continuously as a bounded queue worker

1584	// hostnameOrEmpty returns os.Hostname() or "" on error. Used to populate
1585	// the workerprobe identity envelope without short-circuiting probe startup
1586	// on a transient hostname-lookup failure.

1588		h, err := os.Hostname()

1591		}

1593	}

1595	// runAgentExecuteLoopImpl is the shared implementation for runAgentExecuteLoop
1596	// and runWork. When treatPassthroughAsOpaque is true (ddx work path),
1597	// harness/provider/model are passed to Execute unchanged and
1598	// ValidateForExecuteLoopViaService is not called; DDx does not inspect or
1599	// branch on their values. When false (ddx agent execute-loop path), the
1600	// pre-flight validation gate runs first.
1601	func (f *CommandFactory) runAgentExecuteLoopImpl(cmd *cobra.Command, treatPassthroughAsOpaque bool) error {
1602		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
1603		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
1604		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
1605		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
1606		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
1607		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
1608		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
1609		modelRef, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model-ref")
1610		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
1611		once, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("once")
1612		pollInterval, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("poll-interval")
1613		asJSON, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("json")
1614		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
1615		reviewHarness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-harness")
1616		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")
1617		maxCostUSD, _ := cmd.Flags().GetFloat64("max-cost")
1618		requestTimeout, _ := cmd.Flags().GetDuration("request-timeout")
1619		minPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("min-power")
1620		maxPower, _ := cmd.Flags().GetInt("max-power")
1621	
1622		// Zero-config auto-route: when the operator passes no routing flags and
1623		// the project has no .ddx/config.yaml routing block, dispatch flows
1624		// through fizeau's provider resolution. Provider configuration and any
1625		// "no providers configured" error reporting is fizeau's concern; ddx
1626		// does not read fizeau's config files.
1627		//
1628		// ddx-b2c9a245: tier is inferred per bead via escalation.InferTier in
1629		// the executor closure below when no routing flags are present.
1630		noRoutingFlags := harness == "" && model == "" && provider == "" && modelRef == "" &&
1631			!cmd.Flags().Changed("profile")
1632		autoInferTier := noRoutingFlags && !projectHasRoutingConfig(projectRoot)
1633	
1634		// ADR-022: execute-loop always runs inline. The legacy --local flag is
1635		// a deprecated no-op and the server-submit path is removed from this CLI
1636		// surface — server-spawned workers exec `ddx work` directly.
1637	
1638		// Pre-flight: validate harness availability and model compatibility
1639		// before claiming any beads. This surfaces errors like "claude binary
1640		// not found" or "vidar is an agent preset, not a claude model" before
1641		// any bead status changes hands.
1642		// Skipped for ddx work (treatPassthroughAsOpaque=true): harness/provider/model

1647			}

A2 didn't exceed what A1 did — so the context-limit explanation doesn't fully account for it.

- After the agent commits and has nothing more to do, it should emit task_succeeded and terminate
- Compaction should either succeed (messages_after < messages_before) or bail out cleanly, not retry indefinitely
- If the context genuinely cannot be compacted, the agent should report a structured failure and preserve the commits, not spin

- Detect mid-turn compaction returning success=false repeatedly and bail with a structured 'compaction_stuck' status
- Bound the total number of compaction attempts in a single turn
- Ensure task termination happens immediately after a successful commit + no pending tool calls
- Add a watchdog at the execute-loop level: kill the child if no commit+progress event is observed within a budget
